titleEnglish: Jnana Vijnana Yoga - The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom
description: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 7, Jnana Vijnana Yoga, presents Lord Krishna's teaching on the complete knowledge of the Divine. Krishna reveals His two natures — the lower material nature (earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intellect, ego) and the higher spiritual nature that sustains all life. He declares that everything is strung upon Him like pearls on a thread, and He is the essence in all elements — taste in water, light in the sun and moon, the sacred syllable Om in the Vedas. Krishna explains that His divine Maya, composed of the three gunas, is difficult to overcome, but those who surrender to Him cross beyond it. He describes four types of virtuous devotees who worship Him and declares the jnani (man of wisdom) as dearest to Him. The chapter culminates in the profound truth: Vasudevah Sarvam — Vasudeva (Krishna) is everything.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः।
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु॥
verses.transliteration:
Mayy-asakta-manah partha yogam yunjan-mad-ashrayah
Asamshayam samagram mam yatha jnasyasi tach-chhrinu
verses.hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ! मुझमें आसक्त मनवाला, मेरे शरण होकर योग का अभ्यास करता हुआ, सम्पूर्ण रूप से निःसंदेह जिस प्रकार मुझे जानेगा, वह सुन।
verses.englishTranslation: O Partha, hear how, with your mind attached to Me, practicing yoga and taking refuge in Me, you shall know Me completely and without doubt.
verses.commentary: Krishna opens this chapter by promising Arjuna complete knowledge of the Divine. The prerequisite is a mind attached to God and a life of yogic discipline. This verse sets the tone for the entire chapter — knowledge of God in both His transcendent and immanent aspects.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः।
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
Jnanam te'ham sa-vijnanam-idam vakshyamy-asheshatah
Yaj-jnatva ne-ha bhuyo'nyaj-jnatavyam-avashishyate
verses.hindiTranslation: मैं तुझे ज्ञान और विज्ञान सहित सम्पूर्ण रूप से कहूँगा, जिसे जानकर इस संसार में फिर और कुछ भी जानना शेष नहीं रहता।
verses.englishTranslation: I shall declare to you in full this knowledge along with realization, knowing which nothing further remains to be known in this world.
verses.commentary: Krishna distinguishes between jnana (theoretical knowledge) and vijnana (experiential realization). True spiritual understanding encompasses both intellectual comprehension and direct experience. This is the highest knowledge because it reveals the ultimate reality behind all phenomena.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये।
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः॥
verses.transliteration:
Manushyanam sahasreshu kashchid-yatati siddhaye
Yatatam-api siddhanam kashchin-mam vetti tattvatah
verses.hindiTranslation: हजारों मनुष्यों में कोई एक सिद्धि के लिए यत्न करता है और यत्न करने वाले सिद्धों में भी कोई एक मुझे तत्त्व से जानता है।
verses.englishTranslation: Among thousands of men, scarcely one strives for perfection; and of those who strive and attain perfection, scarcely one knows Me in truth.
verses.commentary: This verse highlights the rarity of true spiritual aspiration and God-realization. Most people are absorbed in worldly pursuits; few seek spiritual perfection. Even among sincere seekers, knowing God in His complete essence is exceedingly rare, underscoring the supreme value of this teaching.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च।
अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा॥
verses.transliteration:
Bhumir-apo'nalo vayuh kham mano buddhir-eva cha
Ahankara iti-iyam me bhinna prakritir-ashtadha
verses.hindiTranslation: पृथ्वी, जल, अग्नि, वायु, आकाश, मन, बुद्धि और अहंकार — ये आठ प्रकार से विभक्त मेरी प्रकृति है।
verses.englishTranslation: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect, and ego — these are the eightfold divisions of My material nature (Prakriti).
verses.commentary: Krishna describes His lower, material nature (apara prakriti) as consisting of eight elements — five gross elements and three subtle ones. This classification encompasses the entire material world, both physical and psychological. Understanding this helps the seeker see all of creation as an expression of the Divine.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम्।
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Apareya-mitas-tv-anyam prakritim viddhi me param
Jiva-bhutam maha-baho yaye-dam dharyate jagat
verses.hindiTranslation: हे महाबाहो! यह (आठ प्रकार की) तो मेरी अपरा (निम्न) प्रकृति है। इसके अतिरिक्त मेरी जीवरूपा परा (श्रेष्ठ) प्रकृति जान, जिससे यह जगत धारण किया जाता है।
verses.englishTranslation: This is My lower nature. But know My other, higher nature — the life-element, O mighty-armed, by which this entire world is sustained.
verses.commentary: Beyond the material nature lies the superior spiritual nature — the conscious principle (jiva) that animates all life. This teaching establishes the distinction between matter and spirit, both of which originate from God. The higher nature is what gives life and purpose to the otherwise inert material elements.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय।
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा॥
verses.transliteration:
Etad-yonini bhutani sarvani-ity-upadharaya
Aham kritsnasya jagatah prabhavah pralayas-tatha
verses.hindiTranslation: सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों की उत्पत्ति इन दोनों प्रकृतियों से है, ऐसा जान। मैं समस्त जगत का प्रभव (उत्पत्ति) और प्रलय (विनाश) हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Know that all beings have their origin in these two natures. I am the source of the entire creation and also its dissolution.
verses.commentary: Krishna establishes Himself as the ultimate cause of both creation and dissolution. The two natures — material and spiritual — together give rise to all living beings. This makes God both the efficient and material cause of the universe, the beginning and end of all existence.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय।
मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव॥
verses.transliteration:
Mattah parataram na-anyat-kinchid-asti dhananjaya
Mayi sarvam-idam protam sutre mani-gana iva
verses.hindiTranslation: हे धनञ्जय! मुझसे परे अन्य कुछ भी नहीं है। यह सम्पूर्ण जगत मुझमें सूत्र में मणियों की भाँति पिरोया हुआ है।
verses.englishTranslation: There is nothing whatsoever higher than Me, O Dhananjaya. All this is strung on Me, as clusters of gems on a string.
verses.commentary: This is one of the most celebrated verses of the Gita. The beautiful metaphor of pearls on a thread illustrates how God is the invisible unifying principle behind all diversity. Just as the thread holds the necklace together while remaining hidden, Krishna pervades and sustains the entire universe while remaining unseen.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः।
प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु॥
verses.transliteration:
Raso'ham-apsu kaunteya prabha-asmi shashi-suryayoh
Pranavah sarva-vedeshu shabdah khe paurusham nrishu
verses.hindiTranslation: हे कौन्तेय! मैं जल में रस हूँ, सूर्य और चन्द्रमा में प्रकाश हूँ, सम्पूर्ण वेदों में प्रणव (ॐ) हूँ, आकाश में शब्द और मनुष्यों में पौरुष हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: I am the taste in water, O son of Kunti; I am the light of the sun and the moon; the syllable Om in all the Vedas; sound in ether; and ability in men.
verses.commentary: Krishna begins revealing His immanent presence in all things by pointing to the essential quality within each element. He is not the gross element itself but its defining essence — that which makes water refreshing, the sun luminous, and the Vedas sacred. This teaches us to perceive God in everyday experience.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ।
जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु॥
verses.transliteration:
Punyo gandhah prithivyam cha tejash-cha-asmi vibhavasau
Jivanam sarva-bhuteshu tapash-cha-asmi tapasvishu
verses.hindiTranslation: मैं पृथ्वी में पवित्र गन्ध हूँ और अग्नि में तेज हूँ। सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों में जीवन हूँ और तपस्वियों में तप हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: I am the pure fragrance of the earth and the brilliance in fire. I am the life in all beings, and the austerity in ascetics.
verses.commentary: Continuing the theme of divine immanence, Krishna identifies Himself with the purest and most essential quality in each category. The fragrance of earth, the heat of fire, and the vitality in all creatures — all point to the same divine source. This vision transforms the mundane into the sacred.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम्।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Bijam mam sarva-bhutanam viddhi partha sanatanam
Buddhir-buddhimatam-asmi tejas-tejasvinam-aham
verses.hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ! सम्पूर्ण प्राणियों का सनातन बीज मुझे जान। मैं बुद्धिमानों की बुद्धि हूँ और तेजस्वियों का तेज हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Know Me to be the eternal seed of all beings, O Partha. I am the intelligence of the intelligent and the splendor of the splendid.
verses.commentary: Krishna declares Himself the primordial seed from which all life springs. He manifests as the highest expression within each category — the intelligence that distinguishes the wise and the radiance that defines the brilliant. This teaches that all exceptional qualities ultimately derive from the Divine.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम्।
धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ॥
verses.transliteration:
Balam balavatam cha-aham kama-raga-vivarjitam
Dharma-aviruddho bhuteshu kamo'smi bharatarshabha
verses.hindiTranslation: हे भरतश्रेष्ठ! बलवानों का काम और राग से रहित बल मैं हूँ और प्राणियों में धर्म के अविरुद्ध काम (इच्छा) मैं हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: I am the strength of the strong, devoid of desire and attachment. I am the desire in beings that is not opposed to dharma, O best of Bharatas.
verses.commentary: Krishna clarifies that He represents strength in its purest form — free from selfish desire and attachment. Similarly, He is righteous desire that aligns with dharma. This nuanced teaching affirms that desire itself is not evil; when channeled righteously, it is a divine expression.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये।
मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि॥
verses.transliteration:
Ye chaiva sattvika bhava rajasas-tamasash-cha ye
Matta eveti tan-viddhi na tv-aham teshu te mayi
verses.hindiTranslation: जो भी सात्त्विक, राजसिक और तामसिक भाव हैं, उन सबको मुझसे ही उत्पन्न जान। किन्तु मैं उनमें नहीं हूँ, वे मुझमें हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Know that all states of being — whether of goodness (sattva), passion (rajas), or ignorance (tamas) — arise from Me alone. Yet I am not in them; they are in Me.
verses.commentary: This profound verse establishes God's transcendence over the three gunas even while being their source. The gunas exist within God, but God is not limited by them. Like a potter who creates pots but is not confined within them, Krishna remains free and untouched by the qualities He generates.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत्।
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Tribhir-guna-mayair-bhavair-ebhih sarvam-idam jagat
Mohitam na-abhijanati mam-ebhyah param-avyayam
verses.hindiTranslation: इन तीनों गुणमय भावों से यह सम्पूर्ण जगत मोहित है, इसलिए इन गुणों से परे अविनाशी मुझे नहीं जानता।
verses.englishTranslation: Deluded by these three qualities of nature (gunas), the whole world does not recognize Me, who am beyond them and imperishable.
verses.commentary: The three gunas act as a veil that prevents most people from perceiving the transcendent Divine. When caught in the interplay of goodness, passion, and ignorance, beings lose sight of the unchanging reality beyond. This delusion is universal and is the fundamental obstacle to spiritual realization.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया।
मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते॥
verses.transliteration:
Daivi hy-esha guna-mayi mama maya duratyaya
Mam-eva ye prapadyante mayam-etam taranti te
verses.hindiTranslation: मेरी यह दैवी त्रिगुणमयी माया अत्यंत दुरत्यय (कठिन) है। परंतु जो मेरी शरण में आते हैं, वे इस माया को पार कर जाते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: This divine Maya of Mine, consisting of the three gunas, is very difficult to overcome. But those who surrender unto Me alone cross beyond this Maya.
verses.commentary: This verse offers both a warning and a promise. Maya is described as divine and nearly impossible to overcome by one's own effort. The only way past it is through complete surrender to God. This establishes bhakti (devotion) as the most effective path to transcend the illusion of material existence.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः।
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः॥
verses.transliteration:
Na mam dushkritino mudhah prapadyante naradhamah
Mayaya-apahrita-jnana asuram bhavam-ashritah

wordByWord.meaning: whose knowledge is stolen by Maya

verses.hindiTranslation: माया द्वारा जिनका ज्ञान हरा जा चुका है, ऐसे आसुरी स्वभाव को आश्रित, मनुष्यों में नीच, दुष्कर्मी और मूढ़ लोग मेरी शरण नहीं लेते।
verses.englishTranslation: The evil-doers, the deluded, the lowest among men, whose knowledge is stolen by Maya, and who partake of a demonic nature — they do not surrender unto Me.
verses.commentary: Krishna describes four types who fail to seek the Divine: the wicked, the foolish, the degraded, and those with demonic tendencies. Their common defect is that Maya has robbed them of discrimination. This verse is not a condemnation but a diagnosis — it explains why some remain turned away from God.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन।
आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ॥
verses.transliteration:
Chatur-vidha bhajante mam janah sukritino'rjuna
Arto jijnasur-artha-arthi jnani cha bharatarshabha
verses.hindiTranslation: हे भरतश्रेष्ठ अर्जुन! चार प्रकार के सुकृती (पुण्यात्मा) मनुष्य मुझे भजते हैं — आर्त (दुःखी), जिज्ञासु, अर्थार्थी (धन चाहने वाला) और ज्ञानी।
verses.englishTranslation: Four kinds of virtuous people worship Me, O Arjuna — the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of wealth, and the man of wisdom, O best of Bharatas.
verses.commentary: All four types of devotees are called virtuous because they turn to God, unlike those described in the previous verse. Each begins the journey from a different motivation — suffering, curiosity, desire for prosperity, or wisdom. Krishna appreciates all of them, for even approaching God with selfish motives is the beginning of spiritual evolution.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते।
प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः॥
verses.transliteration:
Tesham jnani nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir-vishishyate
Priyo hi jnanino'tyartham-aham sa cha mama priyah
verses.hindiTranslation: उनमें नित्य युक्त एकनिष्ठ भक्ति वाला ज्ञानी श्रेष्ठ है; क्योंकि ज्ञानी को मैं अत्यंत प्रिय हूँ और वह मुझे प्रिय है।
verses.englishTranslation: Of these, the man of wisdom, ever united with Me in single-pointed devotion, excels. For I am exceedingly dear to the wise, and he is dear to Me.
verses.commentary: Krishna singles out the jnani — one who worships with pure knowledge and undivided devotion — as the most exalted devotee. The relationship between God and the wise devotee is one of mutual love. This mutual dearness represents the highest form of bhakti, where knowledge and love become inseparable.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम्।
आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Udarah sarva evaite jnani tv-atmaiva me matam
Asthitah sa hi yukta-atma mam-eva-anuttamam gatim
verses.hindiTranslation: ये सब उदार (श्रेष्ठ) हैं, परन्तु ज्ञानी तो साक्षात् मेरा आत्मा ही है — ऐसा मेरा मत है। क्योंकि वह युक्तात्मा मुझ अनुत्तम गति में ही स्थित है।
verses.englishTranslation: All these are noble indeed, but the wise one I regard as My very Self. For, with a steadfast mind, he is established in Me alone as the supreme goal.
verses.commentary: While all four types of devotees are praised as noble, the jnani holds a special place — Krishna considers him as His own Self. This extraordinary statement reveals the ultimate unity between the wise devotee and God. The jnani seeks nothing but God Himself, making the Lord his sole refuge and supreme destination.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते।
वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः॥
verses.transliteration:
Bahunam janmanam-ante jnana-van mam prapadyate
Vasudevah sarvam-iti sa maha-atma su-durlabhah
verses.hindiTranslation: बहुत जन्मों के अंत में ज्ञानवान मनुष्य 'वासुदेवः सर्वम्' — सब कुछ वासुदेव ही है — इस भाव से मेरी शरण लेता है। ऐसा महात्मा अत्यंत दुर्लभ है।
verses.englishTranslation: At the end of many births, the man of wisdom surrenders unto Me, realizing 'Vasudeva is everything.' Such a great soul is very rare.
verses.commentary: This verse contains the pinnacle teaching of Chapter 7: Vasudevah Sarvam — Krishna (Vasudeva) is all that exists. This realization comes after lifetimes of spiritual evolution. The rarity of such a mahatma underscores the supreme value of this vision, where the devotee sees nothing apart from the Divine anywhere.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः।
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया॥
verses.transliteration:
Kamais-tais-tair-hrita-jnanah prapadyante'nya-devatah
Tam tam niyamam-asthaya prakritya niyatah svaya
verses.hindiTranslation: विभिन्न कामनाओं द्वारा जिनका ज्ञान हरा जा चुका है, वे अपने स्वभाव से प्रेरित होकर विभिन्न नियमों का पालन करते हुए अन्य देवताओं की शरण लेते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Those whose knowledge has been carried away by various desires surrender to other gods, following particular rules and rites, driven by their own nature.
verses.commentary: When material desires dominate the mind, people seek fulfillment from lesser deities rather than the Supreme. Their worship is shaped by their inherent tendencies (prakriti). Krishna does not condemn such worship but points out its limitation — it arises from desire rather than true wisdom.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति।
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Yo yo yam yam tanum bhaktah shraddhaya-architum-ichchhati
Tasya tasya-achalam shraddham tam-eva vidadhamy-aham
verses.hindiTranslation: जो-जो भक्त जिस-जिस देवता के स्वरूप को श्रद्धापूर्वक पूजना चाहता है, उस-उस भक्त की श्रद्धा को मैं ही उसी देवता में स्थिर करता हूँ।
verses.englishTranslation: Whatever form a devotee wishes to worship with faith, I make that faith of his firm and steady.
verses.commentary: Krishna reveals His role as the inner director of all faith. Even when devotees worship other deities, it is ultimately Krishna who strengthens their faith. This shows God's infinite compassion — He supports every sincere seeker regardless of the form they worship, knowing all paths eventually lead to Him.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते।
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान्॥
verses.transliteration:
Sa taya shraddhaya yuktas-tasya-aradhanam-ihate
Labhate cha tatah kaman mayaiva vihitan-hi tan
verses.hindiTranslation: वह उस श्रद्धा से युक्त होकर उस देवता की आराधना करता है और उससे अपनी कामनाओं की पूर्ति प्राप्त करता है, जो वास्तव में मेरे द्वारा ही विधान की जाती है।
verses.englishTranslation: Endowed with that faith, he engages in the worship of that deity and obtains his desires, which are in reality granted by Me alone.
verses.commentary: The fruits obtained from worshipping any deity actually come from Krishna alone, the supreme source. The other deities are channels, but the real power behind all blessings is the Supreme Lord. This understanding helps the devotee eventually recognize the one source behind all divine manifestations.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम्।
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि॥
verses.transliteration:
Antavat-tu phalam tesham tad-bhavaty-alpa-medhasam
Devan deva-yajo yanti mad-bhakta yanti mam-api
verses.hindiTranslation: परन्तु उन अल्पबुद्धि वालों का वह फल नाशवान है। देवताओं को पूजने वाले देवताओं को प्राप्त होते हैं, किन्तु मेरे भक्त मुझे ही प्राप्त होते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: But the fruit gained by those of limited understanding is temporary. The worshippers of the gods go to the gods, but My devotees come to Me.
verses.commentary: Krishna contrasts the temporary rewards of worshipping lesser deities with the eternal fruit of devotion to the Supreme. Those who worship demigods attain temporary heavenly pleasures, while devotees of Krishna reach the imperishable Divine. This verse encourages seekers to aim for the highest, most lasting spiritual attainment.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः।
परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Avyaktam vyaktim-apannam manyante mam-abuddhayah
Param bhavam-ajananto mama-avyayam-anuttamam
verses.hindiTranslation: बुद्धिहीन लोग मेरे अव्यय, अनुत्तम, परम भाव को न जानते हुए, मुझ अव्यक्त को व्यक्ति (शरीर) को प्राप्त हुआ मानते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: The unintelligent think of Me, the unmanifest, as having come into manifestation, not knowing My supreme nature which is imperishable and unsurpassed.
verses.commentary: This verse addresses a common spiritual error — seeing God's incarnation as merely a human birth. The unwise fail to recognize that Krishna's divine form is not a product of material nature but an expression of His supreme, imperishable essence. God's manifestation in the world is a voluntary act of grace, not a karmic compulsion.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः।
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Na-aham prakashah sarvasya yoga-maya-samavritah
Mudho'yam na-abhijanati loko mam-ajam-avyayam
verses.hindiTranslation: मैं योगमाया से आवृत हूँ, इसलिए सबके सामने प्रकट नहीं होता। यह मूढ़ संसार मुझ अजन्मा और अविनाशी को नहीं जानता।
verses.englishTranslation: I am not manifest to all, being veiled by My Yoga-Maya. This deluded world does not know Me, the unborn and imperishable.
verses.commentary: Yoga-Maya is God's divine power that conceals His true nature from the unworthy. Unlike the Maya that binds souls, Yoga-Maya is God's voluntary self-concealment. Only through devotion and grace does this veil lift, revealing the eternal, unborn nature of the Supreme. This explains why God-realization is rare despite God being omnipresent.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन॥
verses.transliteration:
Veda-aham samatitani vartamanani cha-arjuna
Bhavishyani cha bhutani mam tu veda na kashchana
verses.hindiTranslation: हे अर्जुन! मैं बीते हुए, वर्तमान और भविष्य के सब प्राणियों को जानता हूँ, परन्तु मुझे कोई नहीं जानता।
verses.englishTranslation: I know all beings of the past, the present, and the future, O Arjuna; but no one knows Me.
verses.commentary: Krishna's omniscience spans all time — past, present, and future — yet He remains unknown to all beings. This asymmetry highlights the vast difference between the infinite Divine consciousness and the limited awareness of individual souls. Only through God's grace can this gap be bridged.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत।
सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप॥
verses.transliteration:
Ichchha-dvesha-samutthena dvandva-mohena bharata
Sarva-bhutani sammoham sarge yanti parantapa

wordByWord.meaning: by delusion of pairs of opposites

verses.hindiTranslation: हे भारत! इच्छा और द्वेष से उत्पन्न द्वन्द्वों के मोह से सम्पूर्ण प्राणी सृष्टि में (जन्म लेते ही) मोहित हो जाते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: By the delusion of the pairs of opposites arising from desire and aversion, O Bharata, all beings fall into ignorance at birth, O scorcher of foes.
verses.commentary: From the very moment of birth, beings are caught in the web of duality — pleasure and pain, like and dislike, heat and cold. This universal delusion, rooted in desire and aversion, prevents recognition of the supreme truth. Understanding this mechanism is the first step toward liberation from it.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम्।
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः॥
verses.transliteration:
Yesham tv-anta-gatam papam jananam punya-karmanam
Te dvandva-moha-nirmuktah bhajante mam dridha-vratah
verses.hindiTranslation: परन्तु जिन पुण्यकर्मी मनुष्यों का पाप समाप्त हो गया है, वे द्वन्द्व-मोह से मुक्त होकर दृढ़ निश्चय से मुझे भजते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: But those people of virtuous deeds, whose sins have come to an end, freed from the delusion of duality, worship Me with firm resolve.
verses.commentary: This verse describes the blessed state of those who have exhausted their negative karma through virtuous living. Free from the confusion of opposites, they worship God with unwavering determination. Their purity of heart and accumulated merit make them eligible for the highest spiritual attainment.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये।
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Jara-marana-mokshaya mam-ashritya yatanti ye
Te brahma tad-viduh kritsnam-adhyatmam karma cha-akhilam
verses.hindiTranslation: जो मनुष्य जरा (बुढ़ापा) और मृत्यु से मुक्ति के लिए मेरा आश्रय लेकर यत्न करते हैं, वे उस ब्रह्म को, सम्पूर्ण अध्यात्म को और सम्पूर्ण कर्म को जान लेते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Those who strive for liberation from old age and death, taking refuge in Me, come to know Brahman fully, the Self, and all about karma.
verses.commentary: By taking refuge in Krishna and striving earnestly, seekers gain complete knowledge of the three great mysteries — Brahman (the Absolute), Adhyatma (the individual self), and Karma (the principle of action). This verse connects devotion with comprehensive spiritual understanding, showing that surrender to God unlocks all wisdom.

verses.speaker: Krishna
verses.devanagari:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः।
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः॥
verses.transliteration:
Sa-adhibhuta-adhidaivam mam sa-adhiyajnam cha ye viduh
Prayana-kale'pi cha mam te vidur-yukta-chetasah
verses.hindiTranslation: जो मुझे अधिभूत, अधिदैव और अधियज्ञ सहित जानते हैं, वे युक्तचित्त मनुष्य प्रयाण (मृत्यु) के समय भी मुझे जानते हैं।
verses.englishTranslation: Those who know Me along with Adhibhuta, Adhidaiva, and Adhiyajna — they, with their minds united with Me, know Me even at the time of death.
verses.commentary: The final verse of Chapter 7 serves as a bridge to Chapter 8, where Arjuna will ask about these very terms. Those who comprehend God in all His aspects — material, divine, and sacrificial — maintain awareness of Him even in the critical moment of death. This steady remembrance at the end of life ensures liberation.
